doc_title: Unauthorized Practice of Law — ABA Model Rule 5.5
label: fabricated
reason: The actual ABA Model Rule 5.5 concerns multijurisdictional practice by licensed lawyers with subsections (a)–(d) on temporary/systematic practice in other jurisdictions, and does not contain the paralegal-focused subsections, definitional p
response_turn1:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 92
REASON: The actual ABA Model Rule 5.5 addresses multijurisdictional practice by lawyers and contains subsections (a) through (d) dealing with temporary and systematic practice in other jurisdictions; it does not contain the subsections shown here about paralegal activities, definitions of "practice of law," or claims about "no recognized emergency exception," which appear to be invented content dressed up in official formatting.
response_turn2:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 92
REASON: The actual ABA Model Rule 5.5 concerns multijurisdictional practice by licensed lawyers with subsections (a)–(d) on temporary/systematic practice in other jurisdictions, and does not contain the paralegal-focused subsections, definitional provisions, or "no emergency exception" language presented here.
